cases.title: Path start
cases.description: Initial camera pose before the auto-path begins moving toward the gallery orb.
cases.communicates: Baseline framing for path-tuning. This is the exact starting pose that the auto-path logic inherits.

cases.title: 20% path progress
cases.description: Auto-path sample at the first evenly spaced progress stop toward the gallery orb.
cases.communicates: Early approach framing. This helps tune acceleration, centering, and early overlay legibility.

cases.title: 40% path progress
cases.description: Auto-path sample at forty percent of the planned approach distance.
cases.communicates: Mid-path stability check. This reveals whether the orb alignment converges cleanly before the close approach.

cases.title: 60% path progress
cases.description: Auto-path sample at sixty percent of the planned approach distance.
cases.communicates: Late-mid path check. This is where motion and portal framing should begin to feel intentional rather than exploratory.

cases.title: 80% path progress
cases.description: Auto-path sample near the close approach to the gallery orb.
cases.communicates: Pre-entry composition check. This shows whether the path gets us near the orb without losing diagnostic clarity.

cases.title: 100% path progress
cases.description: Final pre-entry approach sample at the end of the planned auto-path.
cases.communicates: Terminal path framing. This is the best pre-traversal frame for deciding whether speed, offset, and aim need adjustment.
